evals.prompt: Review this change for PCI-DSS: we added a debug log line in the payment service that logs the full request body of card authorization calls.
evals.expected_output: Critical finding against PCI-DSS 3.2.1 / never-log rules (request body contains PAN/CVV), with remediation (remove or redact, structured logging with field allowlist) and a check of related logging controls.
expectations: Identifies the violation as Critical with the correct control reference (Req 3 / logging rules)
Remediation is concrete: redaction/allowlist, not 'be careful'

evals.prompt: We're a Singapore bank moving our customer notification service to a new cloud region in another country. MAS TRM implications?
evals.expected_output: Review against MAS TRM section 11.5 (cloud, data residency) and Outsourcing Guidelines flag, identifying customer data residency assessment, due diligence, and exit strategy expectations.
expectations: Cites MAS TRM cloud/data expectations (section 11.5 area)
Flags the Outsourcing Guidelines as a related instrument
Asks about or addresses what customer data the service touches

evals.prompt: Compliance check on this diff: new endpoint lets support staff look up customer card numbers for troubleshooting, shows full PAN, no extra auth.
evals.expected_output: Multiple findings: PAN display masking (3.3.1), access control/least privilege (7.2.1), MFA for CDE access (8.4.2), audit logging of access to cardholder data (10.2.1) - with severities and remediations.
expectations: Catches masking, least-privilege, MFA, and audit logging issues - not just one
Each finding carries a specific control ID and severity
Recommends converting findings into backlog stories
